channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Which Embedding Model Should You Actually Use in 2026? I Benchmarked 10 Models to Find Out
item.description: Still using OpenAI's text-embedding-3-small without a second thought? If you're building RAG or vector search systems, you've probably noticed that new embedding models drop every few weeks, each...

item.title: Your AI Agent Has Access to Everything. Who's Watching What It Sends?
item.description: Your AI Agent Has Access to Everything. Who's Watching What It Sends? I'm going to be honest about something: I built mistaike.ai with AI agents (Claude coordinates, Gemini implements), and during...

item.title: Everything That Broke on Day Two
item.description: AI agents don't tell you when they're broken. They exit clean, report success, produce nothing. The first real task went fine. The second one hung for 30 minutes and produced nothing. Day one was...

item.title: PostgreSql(Aggregative Functions)
item.description: Aggregative Function: Aggregate functions perform calculations on multiple rows and return a single result. They’re essential for: Summarizing data (e.g., total salary, average marks) Analyzing...

item.title: pg_duckpipe: Real-time CDC for streaming Postgres Table into Columnar Ducklake
item.description: TL;DR: pg_duckpipe is a PostgreSQL extension that continuously streams your heap tables into DuckLake columnar tables via WAL-based CDC. One SQL call to start, no external infrastructure required....

item.title: 🤖 Adding AI to My Project Wasn't Easy. Until It Was.😅
item.description: Hey fellow developers, 👋 My hyped up hopes and dreams of adding AI magic to my project did not go as planned.🥲 Reality often hurts. 🔍 First Attempt — HuggingFace I asked ChatGPT for free AI model...

item.title: CAIveat Emptor: What You Tell AI Can and Will Be Used Against You
item.description: Article URL: https://natlawreview.com/article/caiveat-emptor-what-you-tell-ai-can-and-will-be-used-against-you Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47452014 Points: 3 # Comments: 0

item.title: 2Checkout API: Ödeme İşleme için Eksiksiz Entegrasyon Rehberi (2026)
item.description: TL;DR 2Checkout API (şimdi Verifone), geliştiricilere RESTful uç noktalar üzerinden ödemeleri işleme, abonelikleri yönetme ve e-ticaret işlemlerini programatik olarak yürütme imkanı sunar. Kimlik...

item.title: 5 Step Guide To Keep Your Checkout Running During Peak Sales Periods
item.description: Two things can happen during big sales events. Your checkout either becomes your strongest revenue driver or your biggest risk. Think about Cyber Monday, Black Friday, Christmas sales, flash...

item.title: The Day a Green Pipeline Told Me Everything Was Fine — And It Wasn't
item.description: By Ashish Nadar — Scholar in Secure Cloud · Operational Excellence · AI-Enabled Software There's a moment every researcher dreads — not the moment when something breaks loudly and obviously, but...

item.title: DoJ Disrupts 3 Million-Device IoT Botnets Behind Record 31.4 Tbps Global DDoS Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Thursday announced the disruption of command-and-control (C2) infrastructure used by several Internet of Things (IoT) botnets like AISURU, Kimwolf,...

item.title: Apple Warns Older iPhones Vulnerable to Coruna, DarkSword Exploit Kit Attacks
item.description: Apple is urging users who are still running an outdated version of iOS to update their iPhones to secure against web-based attacks carried out via powerful exploit kits like Coruna and DarkSword....

item.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked...

item.title: Valve’s huge SteamOS 3.8 update adds long-awaited features — and supports Steam Machine
item.description: Valve has just released SteamOS 3.8.0 in preview, and it's a doozy. Not only is it the first release to support the upcoming Steam Machine living room gaming PC, it comes with long-awaited...

item.title: OpenAI is planning a desktop ‘superapp’
item.description: OpenAI is working on a desktop "superapp" that merges its ChatGPT app, the Codex AI coding app, and its AI-powered Atlas browser into one app, The Wall Street Journal reports. The company is...

item.title: Jeff Bezos reportedly wants $100 billion to buy and transform old manufacturing firms with AI
item.description: The Amazon magnate has a new project centered around acquiring industrial firms and revamping them with AI technology.

item.title: ‘Uncanny Valley’: Nvidia’s ‘Super Bowl of AI,’ Tesla Disappoints, and Meta’s VR Metaverse ‘Shutdown’
item.description: In this episode, we dive into Nvidia’s annual developer conference and what CEO Jensen Huang is saying about the future of the company.

item.title: Cloud service providers ask EU regulator to reinstate VMware partner program
item.description: Broadcom says the group is misrepresenting market "realities."

item.title: Sony’s new WF-1000XM6 earbuds are already on sale for $30 off
item.description: Sony’s WF-1000XM6 are the best noise-canceling wireless earbuds you can buy, but they’ve been hard to recommend over the last-gen model because of the price. While the older version is often on...

item.title: Marc Andreessen is a philosophical zombie
item.description: I admit, this is an innovation I did not see coming: Silicon Valley has invented the philosophical zombie from the classic thought experiment "lol how crazy would it be if there were a...

item.title: The coolest game controller for your phone is $50 off
item.description: The MCON is the closest to a Swiss army knife that a controller can be. No, it doesn’t contain a can opener, but it makes up for it with a surprising array of features. Once you attach your...

item.title: Speagle Malware Hijacks Cobra DocGuard to Steal Data via Compromised Servers
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new malware dubbed Speagle that hijacks the functionality and infrastructure of a legitimate program called Cobra DocGuard. "Speagle is designed to...

item.title: Online bot traffic will exceed human traffic by 2027, Cloudflare CEO says
item.description: AI bots may outnumber humans online by 2027, says Cloudflare CEO Matthew Prince, as generative AI agents dramatically increase web traffic and infrastructure demands.

item.title: Hermès doesn’t include a power adapter with its $5,150 charging case
item.description: Over a decade after Apple first partnered with Hermès for a special Apple Watch collaboration, the French luxury goods maker has released a new collection of leather-wrapped charging accessories...

item.title: 54 EDR Killers Use BYOVD to Exploit 35 Signed Vulnerable Drivers and Disable Security
item.description: A new analysis of endpoint detection and response (EDR) killers has revealed that 54 of them leverage a technique known as bring your own vulnerable driver (BYOVD) by abusing a total of 35...

item.title: A rogue AI led to a serious security incident at Meta
item.description: For almost two hours last week, Meta employees had unauthorized access to company and user data thanks to an AI agent that gave an employee inaccurate technical advice, as previously reported by...

item.title: Microsoft’s go-to Xbox controller is selling at its best price of the year
item.description: Microsoft’s Xbox console strategy needs work, but its Bluetooth-ready controller that shipped alongside the Xbox Series X and Series S is fantastic. We think it’s one of the best Xbox controllers...

item.title: Google Shakes Up Its Browser Agent Team Amid OpenClaw Craze
item.description: As Silicon Valley obsesses over a new wave of AI coding agents, Google and other AI labs are shifting their bets.

item.title: All the wrong EVs are getting canceled
item.description: These past few weeks have been particularly brutal for the EV industry - and anyone who believes that electric vehicles are the future. Thanks to slowing demand and policy whiplashes, automakers...

item.title: Meta rolls out new AI content enforcement systems while reducing reliance on third-party vendors
item.description: Meta believes these AI systems can detect more violations with greater accuracy, better prevent scams, respond more quickly to real-world events, and reduce over-enforcement.
